description: Sensory modifiers for each weave state (stable/thin/frayed/unraveled) applied as overlays to biome descriptors. The LLM combines base biome vocabulary with these modifiers to produce weave-state-aware narration.

stable.description: The weave is intact. Reality works as expected. This is the baseline — no modifications needed.

thin.description: The weave is weakening. Reality is mostly correct but something is subtly wrong. The first sign of the Thinning.

visual: peripheral vision catches things that aren't there
your reflection in water lags by a heartbeat
auditory: sounds carry wrong — conversations from further away than they should
occasional dissonant hum below hearing threshold
echoes where there shouldn't be echoes
birdsong stops for too long, then resumes
olfactory: faint metallic tang underneath normal smells
the smell of wet wool with no source
tactile: the air has a slight static charge
the ground feels less solid — not unsafe, just uncertain

forest: fog that doesn't lift by midday
the path you came in on looks different looking back
plains: the grass ripples against the wind direction
a distant shape on the horizon that's never closer, never further
your shadow points the wrong way for five minutes, then corrects
mountain: echoes come back different from what you said
the ridge line seems to shift when you're not looking
stones are warm where they should be cold
swamp: the water reflects a different sky
sounds of footsteps in the mud when no one else is walking
hills: standing stones hum at frequencies you feel in your teeth
farmland: crops growing unevenly — one row lush, the next withered, no pattern
scarecrows seem to have turned since you last looked
the farmer's dog won't cross a specific field boundary
coast: tide pools reflect stars that aren't in the sky
seabirds avoid a specific stretch of shore
cavern: dripping water falls upward for a heartbeat
the darkness feels thicker than absence of light
crystal formations hum at the edge of hearing
deep_forest: the canopy shifts when you're not watching
bioluminescence pulses in rhythms that feel like breathing
the trees lean toward you, then away
taiga: frost forms in patterns that look deliberate
the gap between trees seems to narrow behind you
tundra: ice cracks in patterns that look like writing
the aurora has colors that don't have names
your breath freezes in shapes that persist too long
desert: heat shimmer forms shapes — buildings, figures — that are too consistent to be mirages
sand moves in circles around specific points
the sun seems to pause for a moment

frayed.description: The weave is failing. Reality is visibly broken. The Fray is present. Environmental hazards, Fray creatures, and active danger.

visual: gravity shifts — light objects drift upward briefly
time stutters — a bird frozen mid-flight, then it's ten feet further
spatial loops — you see yourself walking fifty yards ahead
auditory: sounds from the future — you hear a branch break before it does
voices in languages that don't exist
the sound of weaving — a rhythmic shuttle-thump with no source
silence that presses against your eardrums
olfactory: the smell of burning without fire
something sweet and wrong, like fruit that's fermented inside its skin
tactile: the air has resistance — walking feels like pushing through water
temperature drops twenty degrees in two steps, then returns
the ground gives slightly, like a drum skin

forest: trees growing through each other at impossible angles
clearings that weren't here an hour ago
the canopy showing a different time of day than the ground
mushrooms the size of furniture, luminous and pulsing
plains: the grass is glass — rigid, transparent, chiming when the wind blows
the horizon is closer on one side than the other
sections of ground float six inches above the rest
mountain: the rock face breathes — expanding and contracting visibly
stone formations that weren't here yesterday and won't be here tomorrow
swamp: the water is solid and the ground is liquid in patches
trees growing upside down, roots in air, branches in mud
hills: standing stones have moved — the circle is wrong
peat fires burning without fuel, cold to the touch
the landscape mirrors itself — walk east for an hour, arrive where you started heading west
farmland: crops growing and dying in minutes, cycling
fences that lead to nowhere — they connect fields that don't exist
scarecrows walking, slowly, with no one inside them
rain falling in a perfect circle around one field
coast: the beach is two beaches overlapping — sand and shingle occupying the same space
the sea is glass to the horizon, then violent waves start from nothing
cavern: the cave is bigger inside than outside by orders of magnitude
passages that exist in one direction but not the return
deep_forest: the forest is singing — not birdsong, the wood itself
paths that lead to places that aren't on this continent
the canopy opens to show stars at noon
trees that are also Threadlines, visibly
taiga: snow falls from a clear sky
your footprints in the snow are someone else's boots
the dark between trees is absolute — lanternlight stops at the edge
tundra: the permafrost has become transparent — you can see down into the earth
ice formations that are clearly architectural
desert: the sand is different colors in layers — geological time visible in a surface
oases that don't exist until you look at them
the sun is in two places
sandstorm that moves in a perfect straight line

unraveled.description: The weave has failed. Reality is dissolving. This is the Fray raw. Only the Fray-touched can function here. Everyone else takes constant exposure damage.

visual: reality is layered — you see through the world to what's underneath
geometry doesn't work — parallel lines converge, right angles are wrong
faces appear in surfaces and dissolve
light comes from no direction and casts no shadows
auditory: the sound of the world being unmade — a constant low tearing, like cloth
voices of people who haven't been born yet
your own voice, saying things you haven't said
olfactory: nothing smells like anything — smell has stopped working as a sense
then everything smells like everything at once
then the metallic taste that is the Fray's signature
tactile: your body doesn't trust itself — proprioception is unreliable
touching surfaces is like touching a thought
temperature is a concept, not a sensation
the air is thick enough to leave fingerprints in

forest: the trees are made of Threadline material — visible weave where bark should be
the forest exists in all four seasons simultaneously
the ground is a tapestry you can read if you survive long enough
plains: the grass is Threadlines — millions of them, hair-thin, reaching from the ground to the sky
walking through the Threadline-grass shows you memories — not yours
the horizon doesn't exist — the field goes up
mountain: the mountain is transparent — you can see the Threadlines inside it, the skeleton of reality
the Loom is visible — the metaphysical framework that holds the world together
this is where the Weavers worked
default: the biome has dissolved into raw weave material — no terrain, no features, just the underlying pattern of reality, visible and failing
